content:
# elizaos/eliza Weekly Report (May 24 - 30, 2026)

## 🚀 Highlights
The week of May 24–30 was defined by a major push toward ecosystem maturity, focusing on infrastructure hardening, agent orchestration reliability, and the integration of decentralized identity standards. Key achievements included the implementation of ERC-8004 identity registry support, the introduction of SOC2 compliance controls, and significant refinements to the agentic planner loop to prevent runaway tool-validation and meta-narration errors. The project also successfully expanded its cross-platform reach with multi-architecture ISO support and enhanced CI/CD release integrity, solidifying the framework's stability for both Web2 and Web3 deployments.

## 🛠️ Key Developments

### Agent Orchestration & Planner Logic
Efforts focused on stabilizing the agent's decision-making process and reducing sub-agent overhead.
*   **Planner Reliability:** Addressed Stage 1 self-contradictions and tool-call handling ([#7957](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7957), [#7951](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7951)).
*   **Sub-Agent Management:** Resolved issues with task duplication, loopback URL leakage, and meta-narration of tool results ([#7969](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7969), [#7955](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7955)).
*   **Prompting:** Introduced a new TOON key-value parser to improve prompt evaluation ([#7985](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7985)).

### Infrastructure, Security, & Compliance
The project significantly bolstered its security posture and release pipeline.
*   **Security & Compliance:** Implemented SOC2 verification controls, including Gitleaks scanning and Kubernetes workload checks ([#8014](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8014)).
*   **Release Integrity:** Enhanced the OS release pipeline with SLSA provenance and SBOM verification ([#7906](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7906), [#7907](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7907)).
*   **Multi-Arch Support:** Enabled multi-architecture ISO building (amd64/arm64/riscv64) ([#7971](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7971)).

### Cloud & Ecosystem Integration
*   **Identity & Inference:** Integrated the NEAR AI inference provider ([#7883](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7883)) and implemented ERC-8004 identity registry endpoints ([#7937](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7937)).
*   **Cloud Stability:** Resolved critical 500 errors in the `POST /v1/chat/completions` endpoint ([#7992](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7992)) and replaced HTTP health probes with Redis heartbeats for improved reliability ([#7899](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7899)).

## 🐛 Issues & Triage

### Closed Issues
*   **Safety Policy:** The debate regarding framework-level safety scaffolds for personal-crisis topics was resolved ([#7925](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7925)); maintainers concluded that safety overrides should be handled via per-agent character configuration rather than global defaults.
*   **Ecosystem Plugins:** Successfully integrated community-led plugins, including `plugin-prflght` (Solana firewall), `plugin-gblin` (Base treasury management), and the AgentAudit AI compliance layer ([#7938](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7938), [#7939](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7939), [#7974](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7974)).

### New & Active Issues
*   **CLI Stability:** New issues were opened regarding `@elizaos/cli` boot crashes and global installation failures due to package path export errors ([#8001](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8001), [#8000](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8000)).
*   **Planner Meta-Narration:** Issue [#7960](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7960) remains active, where the planner occasionally narrates tool results instead of returning raw data. Contributor Rajathacharya24 has stepped in to address this.

## 💬 Community & Collaboration
The project continues to see strong community engagement, particularly regarding the expansion of the plugin ecosystem. The successful integration of community-proposed plugins like `plugin-prflght` and `plugin-gblin` demonstrates a healthy collaborative model where external contributors are actively shaping the framework's capabilities. Furthermore, the transition of complex issues like the planner meta-narration to community contributors highlights an active and technically capable developer base.
